headlines.title: Can you keep your kids off school or refuse to work during a heatwave?
headlines.description: As temperatures increase significantly over the next few days, these are your rights.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Questions multiply for the man tipped to replace Starmer
headlines.description: Andy Burnham will have to set out his stall to show how he will deliver on his promises, the BBC's political editor writes.

headlines.title: Is watching football bad for your health? I tested my body to find out
headlines.description: Watching football is an emotional rollercoaster - but is it good or bad for your health?

headlines.title: Ashley Cain says he's 'not proud' of sexist language
headlines.description: The former BBC Three presenter says he has changed since the historical tweets were posted.

headlines.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
headlines.description: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

headlines.title: First drug to delay onset of type 1 diabetes made available on NHS
headlines.description: The immunotherpay can give children and adults three extra years before they need to use insulin.

headlines.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
headlines.description: The note from the possible kidnappers reportedly stated that they did not mean for her to die and included an apology to the family.

headlines.title: Around 29,000 lightning strikes light up sky across southern England
headlines.description: Thunderstorms caused flash flooding and travel disruption across parts of England, with more extreme heat expected on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Tips to keep your home and yourself cool in hot weather
headlines.description: Six simple things you can do to help keep your house cool when temperatures rise.

headlines.title: How to stay safe swimming outdoors - and still have fun
headlines.description: Pick designated swimming spots, learn about riptides and don't use inflatables at the beach, experts say.

headlines.title: From Boston to Miami: Tartan Army adjusts to World Cup culture shock
headlines.description: Scotland football fans are set for a different experience after travelling to Miami, Florida.

headlines.title: England boss Tuchel not a fan of hydration breaks
headlines.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel admits he is not a fan of the controversial hydration breaks being used at the World Cup.

headlines.title: A Golden Boot race for the ages - but who will come out on top?
headlines.description: It is fast turning into a Golden Boot race for the ages as the world's best strikers star at the World Cup. But who will come out on top?

headlines.title: New pictures show dozens of items bought illegally by Peter Murrell
headlines.description: The images include a range of  luxury bags, watches and pens, as well as fresh pictures of a £124,550 motorhome.

headlines.title: How 100 hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
headlines.description: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

headlines.title: Energy bills, defence and social care: What are Burnham's potential policies?
headlines.description: After winning the Makerfield by-election, we look at what policies Andy Burnham has advocated - including transport, education and energy.

headlines.title: Do you know your 'sweat score'? The rise of hydration tech
headlines.description: Hydration tracking gadgets are flooding the market but is it too much information?

headlines.title: 'They made my mum give me up because she was unmarried'
headlines.description: Reg Barker, 66, says he only found out he was adopted when he applied for a passport, aged 18.

headlines.title: Reflecting Pool to be drained as Trump again blames 'vandals' for recent troubles
headlines.description: Trump said vandals cut a long slit in it and possibly dumped fertiliser in the water, and later threatened to sue over reporting on the pool.

headlines.title: Millions in UK could claim share of £3bn after Apple case given green light
headlines.description: Apple rejected the suggestion its practices are anti-competitive, saying many customers rely on third-party alternatives.

headlines.title: National Portrait Gallery display withdrawn after Churchill row
headlines.description: The video installation prompted a row over its claims about Churchill's role in the Bengal famine.

headlines.title: Man returns fly-tipper's rubbish to his doorstep after sifting through CCTV
headlines.description: Dean Gauci tracked down the waste offender using amateur detective skills and returned the sacks.

headlines.title: Ticket reseller StubHub UK customers to get refunds over illegal hidden fees
headlines.description: The Competition and Markets Authority said StubHub must refund 50,000 customers and pay a fine.

headlines.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
headlines.description: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was  killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

headlines.title: Killed train driver was well-known community figure
headlines.description: Tributes are paid to Shaun Burton, who was killed in the crash near Bedford on Friday.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Keir Starmer Resigns as Prime Minister
headlines.description: Keir Starmer resigns as PM, and Andy Burnham confirms he will run to replace him.

headlines.title: The Climate Question: Is climate change ruining our sleep?
headlines.description: How the rise in night-time temperatures is starting to disrupt our sleep and health

headlines.title: How do you solve a problem like Ronaldo?
headlines.description: Portugal take on Uzbekistan with Cristiano Ronaldo in poor form for his country and a backlash raging on social media against his team-mates.

headlines.title: Anderson's journey to England star and potential British record
headlines.description: Elliot Anderson has emerged as a key part of Thomas Tuchel's England side, and could be on the verge of a British record transfer.

headlines.title: A Golden Boot race for the ages - but who will come out on top?
